metadata.title: Why a Spin Flips Completely When You Whisper to It
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = spin-up population (starts full, drains, recovers); CRIMSON = spin-down population (starts empty, fills, drains); the two curves sum to one and cross at 1/2
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no eigenstate-expansion algebra on screen, no interaction-picture formalism, no pulse-area calculation. Mechanism only: the beat between two energy eigenstates drives the Rabi oscillation — populations exchange at the Rabi frequency.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Point a spin up. Switch on a sideways magnetic field. The spin doesn't just wobble — it marches all the way down to spin-down, and then all the way back up. Over and over, at a perfectly regular rate.

props.command: How can a weak resonant drive flip a spin completely?
props.segment: Why a Spin Flips Completely When You Whisper to It
output: Pulse duration sets the rotation angle.

card.copy: Why a Spin Flips Completely When You Whisper to It

beats.narration_text: In the ideal NMR picture, a spin starts along the main field. A coherent radio pulse exactly on resonance, though weak compared with the main field, can flip it completely. The spin-up probability reaches zero after the right pulse duration. That is resonant Rabi oscillation.
The populations exchange sinusoidally. Spin-up starts at one, falls to zero at half a period, rises back to one. Spin-down mirrors it exactly. The two probabilities sum to one at all times.

The question: the transverse drive is weak compared with the main field. Why can it still produce a complete flip? A weak torque makes a slow change, but if phase coherence persists, that small rotation accumulates for as long as the pulse stays on.

card.copy: A weak transverse field completely flips the spin. Small perturbation — maximum change. Why?

beats.narration_text: The naive reading confuses rate with reach. A weak resonant field rotates slowly, so a short pulse barely nudges the spin. But a longer pulse can continue the same coherent rotation all the way to the opposite pole.

The mechanism: the sideways field is not acting on the spin-up and spin-down states directly. It defines two energy eigenstates — spin-x-plus and spin-x-minus — that are equal superpositions of up and down. The spin-up state is a mixture of both.

Each eigenstate accumulates a phase at its own energy rate. The two phases advance at slightly different speeds. Their difference — the beat — grows from zero to pi over half a Rabi period. That phase difference is what drives the spin.

When the beat reaches pi, the two phasors are exactly out of step. They destructively interfere for spin-up and constructively interfere for spin-down. The spin-up probability vanishes. Then the beat continues, the interference reverses, and spin-up recovers.

On resonance, the Rabi angular frequency equals the drive-induced dressed-state splitting divided by h-bar. A weaker drive means a smaller splitting and a longer pi-pulse. Full inversion is possible in this ideal coherent limit. Detuning or decoherence reduces the transfer.

Your turn. An ideal resonant drive has Rabi frequency 20 kilohertz. How long is a pi-pulse? Half a Rabi cycle: 25 microseconds. Continue for another 25 microseconds and the spin returns to up. Weak drive changes the clock, not the reachable pole.

Why can a weak field flip a spin completely? On resonance, it defines two dressed eigenstates that accumulate relative phase. Their beat rotates the state across the Bloch sphere. Drive strength sets how fast; resonance and coherence determine whether full transfer is reached.

card.copy: Transverse field  →  two eigenstates beat.  Beat reaches π  →  complete flip.  Rabi frequency = energy gap / ℏ.
